items.title: map, unordered_map and set: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? map operator[] is a read-only lookup that leaves the container unchanged when a key is absent.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. map operator[] inserts a value-initialized mapped value for a missing key; use find, contains, or at when insertion is not intended.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. map operator[] inserts a value-initialized mapped value for a missing key; use find, contains, or at when insertion is not intended.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

answer.explanation: map operator[] inserts a value-initialized mapped value for a missing key; use find, contains, or at when insertion is not intended.

items.title: map, unordered_map and set: executable application
items.prompt: Read up to 30 whitespace-delimited words, count them in std::map, and print key=count pairs in key order separated by single spaces.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.
